name: Unified landing page and IA shell

mustNotRequire: package-registry network access after dependencies and pnpm-lock.yaml are present

frameworkHardBlockers: GitHub Pages hosting cannot be satisfied
accessible static fallback cannot be satisfied
fixableNonBlockers: dependency install or lockfile setup failure
internal Markdown/MDX route or anchor error

nextActions.missingPnpm: Stop setup, enable or install pnpm locally, then rerun cd docs-site && pnpm install; do not switch to root npm/yarn or a root workspace.
nextActions.installFailure: Fix docs-site package manifest, selected versions, registry access, or lockfile refresh, then rerun pnpm install from docs-site.
nextActions.checkFailure: Fix Astro diagnostics, TypeScript/content typing, or schema issues, then rerun pnpm check.
nextActions.buildFailure: Fix docs-site Astro/Starlight config, package scripts, content/frontmatter/schema, route files, sidebar slugs, or Pages config, then rerun pnpm build and pnpm validate.
nextActions.linkValidationFailure: Fix internal Markdown/MDX routes, anchors, trailing slashes, same-site URLs, or base-path assumptions, then rerun pnpm validate:links.
nextActions.pagesMismatch: Align site, base, trailingSlash, Starlight route slugs, and internal links with GitHub Pages project-page values, then rerun pnpm build and pnpm validate:links; do not add a publish workflow.
nextActions.hardBlocker: Record blocker evidence in plan/research/workflow and apply fallbackOrder only after local fixes are attempted or ruled out.

mustInclude: one static next action per platform
mustAvoid: full content owned by DOC-003 through DOC-010

routeShellContentMinimum.appliesTo: routes whose fullContentOwnerDoc differs from DOC-002 or names multiple owner DOCs
mustInclude: one static next step or related route link
mustAvoid: search, accessibility, or deployment hardening depth

mustInclude: critical platform choices as static native links
source-vs-generated-payload explanation as static semantic content
route metadata visible in page content, not only frontmatter
descriptive link text for platform choices, evidence, and related routes
heading hierarchy that exposes route purpose, ownership, evidence, and next steps
keyboard-reachable links and controls in meaningful source order
visible focus preservation for Starlight defaults and any DOC-002 custom styling
non-color-only meaning for callouts, labels, and status distinctions
static Markdown/MDX fallback for future DOC-006 enhancement points
mustAvoid: JavaScript-only platform choice or route orientation
custom widgets for DOC-002 core navigation
color-only source-vs-payload or route status meaning
responsive layouts that change the reading order of critical content
automated accessibility tooling or responsive screenshot policy owned by DOC-010
accessibilityShellContract.laterOwner: DOC-010 owns automated accessibility checks and responsive/browser hardening after DOC-006 enhancements exist

routes.label: Start
routes.purpose: Explain Racecraft Public Plugins, speckit-pro, platform choice, and first next step.
routes.successCriterion: User chooses Claude Code or Codex path within one screen.

routes.label: Install: Claude Code
routes.purpose: Orient users to adding the marketplace, installing or updating speckit-pro, and verifying namespaced plugin skills.
routes.successCriterion: User reaches a working /speckit-pro:* command path.

routes.label: Install: Codex
routes.purpose: Orient users to adding or selecting the marketplace, installing the plugin, restarting, and verifying custom agents.
routes.successCriterion: User reaches a working $speckit-* flow with custom agents loaded when needed.

routes.label: First Run
routes.purpose: Orient users to one safe first speckit-pro workflow with checkpoints and lifecycle context.
routes.successCriterion: User produces or identifies the expected first artifact and validation checkpoint.

routes.label: Choose Your Path
routes.purpose: Provide a platform and scope selector handoff without browser-side local command execution.
routes.successCriterion: User gets commands relevant only to selected platform and scope, with static fallback.

routes.label: Reference
routes.purpose: Index commands, skills, manifests, marketplace files, hooks, agents, payloads, tests, and file layout.
routes.successCriterion: Each supported surface has a stable deep link and source citation.

routes.label: Troubleshooting
routes.purpose: Orient users to diagnosing install, path, cache, permission, version, and prerequisite failures.
routes.successCriterion: User can identify likely cause and next file or command to inspect.

routes.label: Security & Trust
routes.purpose: Orient evaluators to marketplace trust, generated payloads, hooks, MCP, agents, sandboxing, updates, and rollback.
routes.successCriterion: Evaluator can approve, reject, or ask a concrete follow-up.

routes.label: Contribute & Release
routes.purpose: Orient maintainers to source edits, payload rebuilds, marketplace sync, tests, release-please, and PR expectations.
routes.successCriterion: Maintainer can complete a release-readiness checklist.

routes.label: Spec Kit Lifecycle
routes.purpose: Explain PRD, roadmap, scaffold, autopilot phases, artifacts, and gates with static fallback.
routes.successCriterion: User can explain what each phase produces and validates.

routes.label: Glossary
routes.purpose: Define marketplace, payload, source tree, skill, agent, hook, cache, constitution, and lifecycle terms.
routes.successCriterion: Support answers can link to exact definitions.
